title: Basecamp Webhook Payload
description: Represents the JSON payload delivered by Basecamp to a registered webhook endpoint when a subscribed event occurs within a project. Every payload includes a unique event ID, the event kind, a timestamp, the full recording object that triggered the event, the person who triggered it, and optional event-specific details.

id.description: Unique identifier for this webhook event delivery

kind.description: Event type descriptor identifying the resource type and action, formatted as {resource}_{action} (e.g., message_created, todo_completed, comment_updated)

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when the triggering event occurred in Basecamp

recording.description: Full JSON representation of the Basecamp resource that triggered the event

creator.description: The Basecamp user whose action triggered the event

details.description: Event-specific contextual metadata. For copy and move events includes a copy object with the duplicated item reference. Contents vary by event kind.

copy.description: Reference to the original recording when this event was triggered by a copy or move operation

Person.description: A Basecamp user profile reference included in webhook payloads

attachable_sgid.description: Signed global ID used to reference this person as an attachment

name.description: Full display name of the person

email_address.description: Primary email address of the person

avatar_url.description: URL to the person's avatar image

created_at.description: Timestamp when the person's account was created

updated_at.description: Timestamp when the person's profile was last updated

admin.description: Whether the person is an account administrator

owner.description: Whether the person is the account owner

client.description: Whether the person is a client user

employee.description: Whether the person is an internal employee

time_zone.description: IANA time zone name for the person's local time zone

Bucket.description: Reference to the Basecamp project containing the recording

Recording.description: A Basecamp content resource such as a message, to-do, document, comment, or card. The exact fields present depend on the resource type indicated by the type field.

visible_to_clients.description: Whether the recording is visible to client users

created_at.description: Timestamp when the recording was created

updated_at.description: Timestamp when the recording was last updated

title.description: Title or summary of the recording

inherits_status.description: Whether this recording inherits its status from a parent recording

type.description: Recording type identifying the kind of Basecamp content

url.description: API URL to retrieve this recording

app_url.description: Web URL to open this recording in the Basecamp application

bookmark_url.description: API URL to bookmark this recording

content.description: HTML-formatted body content for messages, documents, and comments

completed.description: Whether the to-do has been completed (Todo type only)

due_on.description: Due date in ISO 8601 date format (Todo type only)

starts_on.description: Start date in ISO 8601 date format (Todo type only)

assignees.description: People assigned to this to-do (Todo type only)

starts_at.description: Start time for schedule entries (Schedule::Entry type only)

ends_at.description: End time for schedule entries (Schedule::Entry type only)

all_day.description: Whether the schedule entry spans the entire day (Schedule::Entry type only)

filename.description: Original filename of the uploaded file (Upload type only)

content_type.description: MIME type of the uploaded file (Upload type only)

byte_size.description: File size in bytes (Upload type only)

download_url.description: Direct download URL for the uploaded file (Upload type only)

comments_count.description: Number of comments on this recording

boosts_count.description: Number of boosts (reactions) on this recording

position.description: Display order position within the parent container

parent.description: Reference to the parent recording (e.g., the message board containing a message, or the to-do list containing a to-do)
